calendar.matters:
DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Mar-19-2018 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Ray D. Hacke, Esq., T:408.966.1072, for real parties in interest/moving party;
Michael S. Dorsi, Esq. of Ad Astra Law Group LLC, T:415.795.3579, for petitioner/opposing party;
Marc R. Lewis, Esq. of Lewis & Llewellyn, LLP, T:415.800.0591, for petitioner/opposing party.

RULING: The tentative ruling was contested and argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopts the tentative ruling on REAL PARTIES IN INTEREST ARK OF HOPE PRESCHOOL and LUTHERAN CHURCH OF THE HOLY SPIRIT's DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION as follows:

Real Parties in Interest Ark of Hope Preschool and Lutheran Church of the Holy Spirit's demurrer to the petition for writ of mandate filed by petitioner PNB Noriega, LLC is OVERRULED. Real Parties in Interest do not dispute that the Board's decision reversing the Planning Commission's decision required written findings. Because of the requirement of written findings, the sentence of CCP 1094.6(b) that determines when the 90-day period to seek judicial review begins is the second to last sentence of that subsection. Because PNB's petition was filed within 90 days of the date the Board's findings were mailed, the petition was timely filed.

ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, email: torreanom@aol.com; mtorreano@sftc.org.
